message:
feat(index,fusion,fsfs): harden vector indexing, add SearchParams, fix incremental rebuild and watcher event ordering

Four functional improvements across core search infrastructure:

1. FSVI header field validation (frankensearch-index/src/fsvi.rs):
   Reject embedder_id and embedder_revision strings exceeding u8::MAX
   bytes at index creation time, preventing truncation corruption in
   the binary header format. Adds two tests for boundary enforcement.

2. Configurable SearchParams (frankensearch-index/src/search.rs):
   Introduce SearchParams struct exposing parallel_threshold,
   parallel_chunk_size, and parallel_enabled knobs. Add
   search_top_k_with_params() allowing callers to override compiled-in
   defaults. Cache parallel-search env var lookup via OnceLock to avoid
   repeated std::env::var calls on every search. Re-export SearchParams
   from lib.rs. Includes four parameterized search tests.

3. Incremental rebuild preservation (frankensearch-fusion/src/refresh.rs):
   Fix RefreshWorker::run_cycle to merge the current embedding queue
   drain with the previously-built vector index, so documents not in
   the current batch are preserved across incremental cycles instead
   of being silently dropped. De-duplicates within-batch updates by
   keeping only the latest record per doc_id. Updates seed_index()
   helper to use the lower-level VectorIndex::create API. Adds
   incremental_rebuild_preserves_docs_not_in_current_batch test.

4. Out-of-order watcher event fix (frankensearch-fsfs/src/orchestration.rs):
   Fix FsWatcher::enqueue_event to use get_mut instead of insert-then-
   check, preventing newer pending state (e.g., Delete) from being
   overwritten by a stale event (e.g., Modify) that arrived late.
   Adds watcher_out_of_order_event_does_not_overwrite_newer test.

Also cleans up float comparison assertions in pressure.rs tests to use
epsilon-based comparisons instead of assert_eq on f64 values, and fixes
a dead-code warning on DegradationStateMachine::observe return value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
